messages.content: You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Tell the story of this startup: 23andMe

## 23andMe — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Pioneering consumer genetic-testing company faced a major 2023 data breach and never found a profitable business beyond one-time DNA kits, filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in March 2025.

### Lesson
A one-time-purchase consumer product needs a recurring revenue engine to survive beyond the initial hype cycle.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Explain the history and key events: 2U Inc.

## 2U Inc. — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Online program management company that powered university degrees and acquired edX filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after years of stock decline and unsustainable university revenue-share contracts.

### Lesson
Revenue-share contracts with universities can become structurally unprofitable as the market matures.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
What happened to this company and why? 5miles

## 5miles — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Local classifieds and secondhand marketplace app raised heavy funding but was shut down around 2019 after failing to compete with letgo and OfferUp.

### Lesson
Heavily funded but undifferentiated marketplace apps struggle once category leaders consolidate share.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Analyze the founders and timeline: A123 Systems

## A123 Systems — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
MIT-spinoff lithium-ion battery maker won a $249M DOE grant but was crushed by a costly Fisker recall and cheap competition, filing for bankruptcy in 2012.

### Lesson
Battery makers are highly exposed to the fortunes of the small number of automaker customers they depend on.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Summarize the key metrics and narrative: Akili Interactive

## Akili Interactive — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Maker of EndeavorRx, the first FDA-cleared video-game-based digital therapeutic for ADHD, went public via SPAC but struggled commercially and sold its core assets in 2024 after running low on cash.

### Lesson
FDA clearance alone doesn't guarantee payer coverage or consumer willingness to pay.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
What's the lesson from this case? Amazon Care

## Amazon Care — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Amazon's in-house telehealth and home-visit service for employees and enterprise clients was shut down in 2022 as Amazon pivoted its healthcare strategy toward acquiring One Medical.

### Lesson
Even deep-pocketed corporate backers will kill a healthcare bet that doesn't show a path to scale.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Tell the story of this startup: Anki

## Anki — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Maker of consumer robotics toys shut down abruptly after failing to close a final funding round, despite selling over 1.5 million robots.

### Lesson
Consumer robotics toy margins can be too thin to sustain the R&D costs of building lovable hardware.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Self-driving AI startup backed by Ford and Volkswagen shut down after both automakers concluded fully autonomous robotaxi tech was too far from commercial viability.

### Lesson
Deep-pocketed corporate backers can still walk away when a capital-intensive AI bet's timeline stretches too long.

What happened to this company and why? Arrival

## Arrival — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
UK-based electric van and bus maker known for its 'microfactory' manufacturing concept that went public via SPAC in 2021 but filed for bankruptcy in 2024 after failing to reach production at scale.

### Lesson
Novel micro-factory manufacturing concepts still need to prove out at commercial volume before investors' patience runs out.

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Hyperloop-adjacent high-speed ground transportation startup founded by a former Hyperloop One executive that shut down in 2018 after failing to secure a route deal in Colorado.

Infrastructure-dependent transportation startups need a committed government partner before spending down capital on engineering.

Summarize the key metrics and narrative: Azlo

## Azlo — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
No-fee digital banking app for freelancers and small businesses, backed by BBVA, that shut down in 2021 after BBVA sold its US banking operations to PNC and discontinued the unprofitable unit.

### Lesson
A fintech dependent on a single incumbent-bank sponsor is exposed to that sponsor's own strategic pivots.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Retail-as-a-service startup that let D2C brands showcase products in physical stores shut down its US retail operations in 2023 after failing to reach profitability.

### Lesson
Bridging online D2C brands into physical retail carries real estate and staffing costs that are hard to offset.

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
UK-based AI-driven telehealth and symptom-checker company expanded aggressively into US value-based care contracts, burned through cash, and filed for bankruptcy/liquidation in 2023.

### Lesson
Scaling into risk-bearing healthcare contracts without disciplined unit economics can sink even well-funded tech.

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Celebrity-branded subscription commerce startup spanning fashion and beauty brands shut down in 2015 after its multi-brand strategy failed to gain traction.

### Lesson
Launching multiple celebrity-branded subscription lines simultaneously spreads resources too thin to build any one brand well.

## Beepi — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Peer-to-peer used car buying and selling marketplace shut down in 2017 after burning through heavy funding without solving the capital intensity of buying and reconditioning cars.

Used-car marketplaces require far more working capital than typical marketplace models assume.

Analyze the founders and timeline: Bench Accounting

## Bench Accounting — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Online bookkeeping and tax platform for small businesses that abruptly shut down in December 2024, locking out customers from their financial records before its assets were acquired by Employer.com.

### Lesson
Abrupt shutdowns of financial recordkeeping services can cause disproportionate harm to small-business customers relative to the company's own losses.

Summarize the key metrics and narrative: Berkshire Grey

## Berkshire Grey — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Warehouse robotics and fulfillment automation company that went public via SPAC at a rich valuation, then was taken private by SoftBank at a fraction of that price.

### Lesson
A SPAC valuation built on future promise can collapse fast once real-world adoption lags.

What's the lesson from this case? Better Place

## Better Place — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Israeli startup built battery-swap stations and EVs but high infrastructure costs and weak vehicle sales led to bankruptcy.

### Lesson
Building a proprietary infrastructure network before demand exists is extraordinarily expensive and risky.

Tell the story of this startup: Better.com

## Better.com — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Digital mortgage lender that grew rapidly during the pandemic refinancing boom saw its reputation and finances collapse after its CEO fired 900 employees over a Zoom call.

### Lesson
Scaling headcount to match a temporary rate-driven demand spike leaves a company badly overextended once rates rise.

Explain the history and key events: Birchbox

## Birchbox — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Pioneer of the beauty subscription box model struggled for years with churn and cash shortfalls, laying off large portions of staff repeatedly before being sold in a distressed 2023 deal.

### Lesson
Subscription box novelty fades fast once retention costs exceed customer lifetime value.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
What happened to this company and why? Bird

## Bird — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Electric scooter-sharing pioneer that scaled rapidly across hundreds of cities but never achieved sustainable unit economics; filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in December 2023.

### Lesson
Rapid geographic expansion without solving vehicle durability and repair costs burns cash faster than growth can offset.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Analyze the founders and timeline: Bitconnect

## Bitconnect — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Crypto lending platform promising outsized guaranteed returns via a proprietary trading bot shut down after regulators issued cease-and-desist orders, revealing a Ponzi scheme worth over $2B in losses.

### Lesson
Guaranteed fixed returns with an opaque, unaudited strategy are the classic signature of a Ponzi scheme.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
Summarize the key metrics and narrative: BlockFi

## BlockFi — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Crypto lender filed for Chapter 11 after its exposure to FTX/Alameda, which had bailed it out earlier that year, became fatal.

### Lesson
Accepting a rescue loan from a counterparty that is itself fragile just delays and compounds the eventual failure.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
What's the lesson from this case? BloomTech (Lambda School)

## BloomTech (Lambda School) — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Coding bootcamp built on income-share agreements that faced state regulatory actions and an FTC investigation over misleading job-placement statistics, shrinking dramatically.

### Lesson
Income-share-agreement business models invite regulatory scrutiny if outcome claims aren't rigorously verified.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.

## Blue Apron — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Meal-kit subscription pioneer that IPO'd in 2017, struggled with customer acquisition costs and churn for years, and was acquired by Wonder Group in 2023 after its stock collapsed.

### Lesson
High customer acquisition costs paired with poor retention doom subscription commerce even with first-mover advantage.

Explain the history and key events: Bluefly

## Bluefly — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Early online discount fashion retailer filed for bankruptcy in 2018 after two decades of struggling to turn a sustainable profit against larger competitors.

### Lesson
Longevity alone doesn't protect a discount ecommerce retailer without a durable cost or sourcing advantage.
You are a seasoned VC partner analyzing startup case studies.
What happened to this company and why? Bó

## Bó — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Digital-only bank launched by RBS (NatWest) in late 2019 to compete with Monzo and Starling; shut down just six months later in mid-2020 after failing to gain traction and to justify its cost amid COVID-19.

### Lesson
An incumbent bank spinning up a challenger brand still needs genuine differentiation, not just a new app skin.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Electric truck and commercial van startup that filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy liquidation in 2024 after years of delayed production and failed partnership attempts, including with Mullen Automotive.

### Lesson
Commercial EV makers need deep-pocketed manufacturing partners; failed partnership talks can be a fatal blow.

## Bolt Financial — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
One-click checkout SaaS reached an $11B valuation before layoffs and public controversy over founder claims about funding and metrics led to a collapse in its valuation.

### Lesson
Founder credibility problems can destroy enterprise sales momentum even when the core product works.

What's the lesson from this case? Bonobos

## Bonobos — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Menswear D2C ecommerce pioneer was acquired by Walmart in 2017 for $310M, sold to Express in 2023, and its inventory was liquidated after Express filed for bankruptcy in 2024.

A strong D2C brand can still be undone by a series of ownership changes that never find it a stable strategic home.

## Boo.com — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
European luxury fashion ecommerce site spent lavishly on a Flash-heavy website and global launch before collapsing in 2000, becoming a symbol of dot-com excess.

Simultaneous global launch and heavy pre-revenue marketing spend is extremely high risk.

## Bossa Nova Robotics — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Retail shelf-scanning robotics company whose flagship deployment with Walmart ended, after which the company ceased its retail-robotics operations.

### Lesson
Depending on a single anchor retail customer leaves a hardware startup dangerously exposed to one contract decision.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Indian dockless scooter-rental startup (formerly Metro Bikes) that shut down its core rental business in 2022 to pivot fully into electric-vehicle manufacturing.

Two-wheeler rental economics in price-sensitive markets pushed the company to abandon its original business entirely.

## Boxed — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Bulk wholesale ecommerce retailer aimed at non-Costco-member shoppers went public via SPAC in 2022 and filed for bankruptcy in 2024, with assets acquired by rival Sam's Club owner.

Thin-margin bulk retail online struggles to outrun warehouse club incumbents with superior logistics scale.

## Brandless — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
D2C consumer goods retailer selling unbranded household products at flat $3 prices; shut down in 2020 after burning through massive SoftBank-led funding without reaching profitability.

A minimalist branding gimmick cannot substitute for a durable unit-economics advantage.

## Bridj — ✗ FAILED

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

On-demand shuttle bus startup piloted in Boston, Kansas City and Sydney that shut down in 2017 after failing to find a sustainable model and losing a major transit-agency partnership.

Public transit-agency partnerships can be a lifeline or a liability depending on how dependent the startup's revenue is on them.

## Bright Health — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Tech-enabled health insurance startup expanded rapidly into ACA and Medicare Advantage markets nationwide, then exited nearly all insurance lines and sold its remaining businesses by 2024 after massive losses.

### Lesson
Underwriting risk in health insurance punishes growth-at-all-costs expansion into new geographies and plan types.

## Builder.ai — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
AI-powered app-development platform once valued at $1.5B collapsed into insolvency amid revelations that much of its 'AI' output relied on human engineers.

Overstating AI automation capability to investors and customers eventually collapses under financial scrutiny.

## Bulletin — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Wholesale marketplace connecting independent brands with retail shelf space shut down in 2020 as the pandemic collapsed brick-and-mortar retail demand.

### Lesson
A marketplace tied to physical retail placement is acutely vulnerable to shocks in offline retail.

## Byju's — ✗ FAILED

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Once the world's most valuable edtech company, Byju's collapsed under aggressive debt-fueled acquisitions, accounting irregularities, and auditor/board resignations, leading to insolvency proceedings.

### Lesson
Aggressive debt-fueled M&A without financial controls can sink even category-leading startups.

## Byte — ✗ FAILED

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Vine co-creator Dom Hofmann's short-video app reboot launched to fanfare in 2020 but couldn't compete with TikTok's scale and was shut down in 2023.

Nostalgia for a beloved defunct product doesn't overcome a much larger incumbent's head start.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

China-based EV startup founded by ex-BMW and Nissan executives that showcased a futuristic SUV concept for years but never reached mass production and formally dissolved in 2023.

Years of flashy concept demos can't substitute for an actual production line and delivered vehicles.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

A crowded, fast-moving category can commoditize a startup's advantage within a couple of years.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

A distinctive platform design doesn't substitute for the manufacturing capital needed to actually build cars at scale.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

Free-floating car-sharing fleets require fleet-scale capital that even automakers found unsustainable long term.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

A consignment marketplace model for cars still carries heavy inventory and reconditioning costs.

### Timeline
(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

### Narrative
Bed-in-a-box mattress D2C pioneer IPO'd in 2020 at a fraction of its earlier private valuation, then was taken private in 2022 by Durational Capital at a steep discount.

A category creator can still lose once dozens of well-funded copycats commoditize the product.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

A hot IPO can mask a business model that never found strong enough retention or pricing power.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

Yields that outpace any plausible underlying return are usually funded by new depositors, not real profit.

(No timeline data)

### Key Metrics
(No metrics)

Growth built on liberal controlled-substance prescribing invites regulatory intervention that can gut a company almost overnight.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

Generative AI can rapidly commoditize a business built on giving students step-by-step answers.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

In a saturated market, being acquired by a larger rival is often the only viable exit.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

(No timeline data)

(No timeline data)

(No timeline data)

(No timeline data)

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)

Demand pulled forward by a crisis can vanish just as fast, stranding capacity built for peak conditions.

(No timeline data)

(No metrics)
